title: Single-cell dissociation from zebrafish larvae
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cgitr","text":"This protocol describes a robust and reproducible method for generating single-cell suspensions from zebrafish larvae (96 and 120 hpf) for downstream single-cell analysis, such as the 10X Genomics Chromium platform. The procedure includes enzymatic digestion using TrypLE and Collagenase/Dispase in a supplemented HBSS buffer, followed by mechanical dissociation and multiple filtration and washing steps. Critical quality control steps ensure cell viability (\u003e80%) and concentration (\u003e400 cells/μL), which are essential for high-quality single-cell sequencing. This SOP emphasizes the use of standardized reagents, precise timing, and temperature-controlled equipment to ensure consistency across samples. The workflow is optimized for processing up to three samples simultaneously and includes detailed buffer compositions, cell viability assessment using Trypan Blue, and guidelines for chip loading. This method enables high-throughput and reproducible single-cell transcriptomic analysis from whole zebrafish larvae, facilitating developmental and toxicological studies at single-cell resolution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ehiap","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oolt","text":"Sample before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"nuftn","text":"- More than 3 samples are difficult to handle and not recommended.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egrtx","text":"- Always use fresh larvae from 28°C incubator","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wvjf0","text":"- For 96 hpf and 120 hpf larvae, pool 5 individuals per sample","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pwhuu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uym4m","text":"Before you start:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"krw31","text":"- The working surface of the safety cabinet must be disinfected","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m495f","text":"- Pre-cool centrifuge to 4°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hfi2u","text":"- Pre-warm Thermomixer to 28°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fsjtd","text":"- Wear a lab coat and gloves for all working steps","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m5wym","text":"- Prepare a LabTrackingForm to note cell preparation information","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Collagenase/Dispase Prepare in advance. Resuspend the Collagenase/Dispase in nuclease-free water to 100mg/mL. Prepare single-use aliquots ( 45 µL ) and store them at -20 °C or -80 °C . Once thawed, do not refreeze/reuse.

Supplemented HBSS Prepare in advance, store at 4 °C , and warm to 28 °C before the start of the experiment. A B C 1 Ingredient Final concentration Amount 2 HBSS - 49225 μL 3 CaCl2 0.5 nM 25 μL 4 HEPES 15 mM 750 μL
Dilution buffer Prepare fresh and keep On ice until use. A B C 1 Ingredient Final concentration Amount 2 HBSS (unsuppl.) - 496 μL 3 BSA 0.04% 4 μL
Washing buffer Prepare fresh and keep On ice until use. %s %s
Quenching buffer Prepare fresh and keep at 28 °C until use. %s %s
Prepare fresh and keep at 28 °C until use. Add Collagenase/Dispase just directly before usage. %s %s
Perform the following working steps at Room temperature :
Perform the following working steps under a safety cabinet:
Perform the following working steps under a safety cabinet:
Perform the following working steps under a safety cabinet:
Perform the following working steps under a safety cabinet:
Perform the following working steps under a safety cabinet:
Visually check the cell suspension using a microscope.
Perform the following working steps in a dust-free environment.
Cleaning
Obtain zebrafish larvae from a 28 °C incubator (do not place larvae on ice!).
Per sample, transfer five larvae into fresh 1.5 mL LowBind SafeLockTubes with some cultivation water.
Place tubes with collected larvae in the Thermomixer at 28 °C .
Carefully take off water without pipetting the larvae and immediately add 1 mL of supplemented and pre-warmed HBSS.
Carefully take off 900 µL HBSS without pipetting the larvae.
Add TrypL and Collagenase/Dispase to Dissociation-Mix.
Carefully take off the rest of HBSS without pipetting the larvae using a 300 μL pipette tip.
Immediately add 500 µL of prewarmed Dissociation buffer.
Incubate larvae at 28 °C for 00:03:00 .
Using a 1 mL tip, pipette up and down the larvae 20 times.
Incubate larvae at 28 °C 28 °C for 00:03:00 .
Using a 1 mL tip, carefully pipette up and down the larvae 15 times.
Repeat the pipetting/incubation step every 3 minutes for 5 cycles (15 minutes).
Add 500 µL Quenching buffer per dissociated ZFE (per tube), and mix by careful pipetting using a normal 1 mL tip 10 times.
Immediately strain the cells carefully through a 70 μM FlowMi pipette tip cell strainer into a fresh 1.5 mL LowBind SafeLockTube.
0 µL Spin down cells for 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes (acceleration set to 7 and brake to 8) and place on ice.
Carefully take off the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Add 1 mL of prechilled washing buffer to the cell pellet.
Carefully resuspend cells by pipetting them up and down using a wide-bore pipette tip and placing them on ice.
Immediately strain the cells carefully through a 40 μM FlowMi pipette tip cell strainer into a fresh 1.5 mL LowBind SafeLockTube.
Spin down cells for 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes (acceleration set to 7 and brake to 8) and place on ice.
Carefully take off the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Add 100 µL of prechilled dilution buffer to the cell pellet (total volume ~ 120 μL).
Carefully resuspend cells by pipetting them up and down using a wide-bore pipette tip and placing them on ice.
Start cell counting and cell viability estimation.
Transfer 10 µL Trypan-Blue into a fresh 1.5 mL LowBind SafeLockTube.
Add 10 µL of cell suspension to Trypan-Blue.
Mix by carefully flicking.
Apply 10 μL of the stained cell suspension to a Neubauer counting chamber.
In four quadrants, count alive and dead cells separately and note the results in the LabTrackingForm.
Take a picture using the microscope camera for documentation.
Calculate the viability rate.
Apply 10 μL of the unstained cell suspension to a Neubauer counting chamber.
Count all cells in four quadrants and note the LabTrackingForm results.
Take a picture using the microscope camera for documentation.
Calculate the cell number per μL.
Visually check the cell suspension for undissociated tissue and debris. It should be as clean as possible.
Check if cell viability is sufficient for the 10X single-cell workflow (> 80%).
Check if the cell concentration is sufficient for the 10X single-cell workflow (>400 cells per µL).
Prepare the 10X Chip and MasterMix according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Strain the cell suspension (~100 μL) carefully through a 40 μM FlowMi pipette tip cell strainer into a fresh 1.5 mL LowBind SafeLockTube.
Add cell suspension to MasterMix and start Chip loading.
Clean all used materials and return them to their original place.
Clean and disinfect the safety cabinet.
Turn off all used instruments.
creator: Stephan Schreiber
